frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment:
Sunita: Kal Sharma ji ki beti ko dekha tha na, simple salwar-suit mein kitni sabhya lag rahi thi. Aajkal ki ladkiyan jeans-top mein khud hi itna dhyan kheench leti hain, phir bolti hain log dekhte kyun hain.
Husband: Haan, apne kapdon mein thodi maryada ho toh ladki ki parvarish bhi dikhti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's clothing with her character, family upbringing, and responsibility for male attention. It encodes the 'traditional = respectable' framing and subtly blames harassment on non-traditional dress.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married. A homemaker in Lucknow. Your 23 year old daughter Priya has started wearing makeup to college Ñ foundation, eyeliner, lipstick. Nothing extreme but noticeably more than before. You are sitting with your husband after dinner after Priya has gone to her room. You are telling your husband what you have noticed about Priya lately and what you think the makeup means and what kind of attention she is trying to attract and whether this is the kind of girl you raised her to be.
